sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.95 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a permutation group on the finite set$$. A partition$$of$$is said to be %section-regular\/ or$G$-regular if there exists a subset$S$of$$such that$S^g$is a %section (also called a transversal %) of$$for all$g G$. The group$G$is said to be synchronizing if$| | > 2$and there are no non-trivial proper$G$-regular partitions on$$. a) Are the following affine-type primitive groups synchronizing:...
